properties.remark: MESONET STATION XBOC AT BOCA RATON REPORTED A 40 MPH/35 KT WIND GUST AT 1:33 PM.
MESONET STATION XHBI AT HILLSBORO INLET REPORTED A 39 MPH/34 KT WIND GUST AT 1:38 PM.
41 MPH GUST MEASURED AT LAKE OKE WEST END SITE (L005).
39 MPH GUST MEASURED AT LAKE OKE CENTER (LZ40) SITE.
VIDEO OF A WATERSPOUT OFF COAST OF FISHER ISLAND RELAYED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.

THE CITY OF MIAMI POLICE DEPARTMENT REPORTED FLOODING ACROSS MULTIPLE ROADWAYS IN DOWNTOWN MIAMI DUE TO HEAVY RAINFALL IN THE AREA. SOME OF THESE INCLUDE NW 5TH AVE & 22ND ST, BISCAYNE BLVD TO BAYSHORE/NE 10-25 ST, N FEDERAL HWY & 50-52ND ST. CARS STRANDED ALONG SECONDARY STREETS NEAR I-395 NEAR BISCAYNE BLVD.
NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED FLOODING ACROSS NUMEROUS ROADWAYS IN PEMBROKE PINES DUE TO HEAVY RAINFALL IN THE AREA. NUMEROUS ROADS HAD ONLY ONE LANE PASSABLE.
MESONET STATION XGVT GOVERNMENT CUT REPORTED A WIND GUST OF 43MPH AT 10:52AM AS A GUSTY SHOWER MOVED ONSHORE. STATION IS ELEVATED WITH A HEIGHT OF 75 FEET.

FIU POLICE PROVIDED A REPORT OF SIGNIFICANT FLOODING AT THE FIU BISCAYNE BAY CAMPUS. THIS IS SIMILAR TO OTHER REPORTS FROM THE AREA. RAINFALL OF ABOUT 4-5 INCHES HAVE BEEN RECORDED FROM THE GENERAL AREA SO FAR TODAY.
MINIMAL DAMAGE WAS NOTED IN A VERY SMALL AREA. THE TORNADO MAY HAVE JUST IMPACTED THE TREE-TOP LEVEL. A WEAK TORNADIC DEBRIS SIGNATURE (TDS) WAS NOTED ON RADAR IN THE EXACT LOCATION OF THE DAMAGE.
REPORT FROM MPING: STREET/ROAD FLOODING; STREET/ROAD CLOSED; VEHICLES STRANDED.
EMERGENCY MANAGER FROM SUNRISE REPORTED A VEHICLE RESCUE IN DEEP WATER IN THE PARKING LOT OF SAWGRASS MILLS MALL. RAINFALL REPORTS OF 6-7 INCHES HAVE BEEN MEASURED SO FAR TODAY.
VIDEO FROM BROADCAST MEDIA SHOWS THE OUTER LANES OF SR 84 IMPASSIBLE FROM DEEP WATER. RAINFALL AMOUNTS IN THE AREA OF ABOUT 8 INCHES HAVE BEEN RECEIVED SO FAR TODAY.

PICTURES CONTINUE TO BE RECEIVED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA OF SEVERE FLOODING ON EAST PERIMETER ROAD AT FORT LAUDERDALE/HOLLYWOOD INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT, WITH WATER CLOSE TO THE TOPS OF CAR TIRES. UNOFFICIAL RAINFALL MEASUREMENTS OF AT LEAST 10 INCHES HAVE BEEN RECEIVED FROM THE AREA SO FAR TODAY.

VIDEO OF FLOODING ON BROWARD BOULEVARD NEAR SE 1ST AVE IN DOWNTOWN FT. LAUDERDALE. OUTER LINES APPEARED TO BE IMPASSIBLE FROM DEEP WATER. UNOFFICIAL RAINFALL REPORTS OF 6-8 INCHES HAVE BEEN RECEIVED FROM THE AREA.

A TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN JUST SOUTH OF KFLL AND MOVED ALMOST DUE WEST FOR ABOUT A HALF OF A MILE. THE TORNADO CAUSED TREE DAMAGE ALONG OLD GRIFFIN ROAD BEFORE CROSSING INTO A MOBILE HOME COMMUNITY EAST OF INTERSTATE 95. METAL SIDING ROOF DAMAGE WAS NOTED THROUGHOUT THE COMMUNITY. THE EFO WAS RATED ON THE HIGH-END OF THE SCALE BASED ON THIS DAMAGE. A TORNADIC DEBRIS SIGNATURE (TDS) WAS ALSO NOTED ON RADAR WITH THIS TORNADO.
EARTHNETWORKS BPJSM BELEN JESUIT PREPARATORY SCHOOL MESONET REPORTED A WIND GUST OF 61MPH AT 4:24PM AS A SEVERE THUNDERSTORM MOVED THROUGH THE AREA. SITE ELEVATION IS 53 FEET.
SOCIAL MEDIA REPORT PHOTO OF A LARGE TREE UPROOTED/KNOCKED DOWN ACROSS THE STREET FROM WESLEY MATHEWS ELEMENTARY SCHOOL AS A SEVERE THUNDERSTORM MOVED THROUGH THE AREA.

EMERGENCY MANAGER AT PALM BEACH COUNTY REPORT WIND DAMAGE TO PRIVATE AND PUBLIC PROPERTY NEAR OKEELANTA, FL DUE TO SEVERE THUNDERSTORM. DAMAGE INCLUDES OVERTURNED TRAILERS AND PORTABLE BATHROOMS, BROKEN FENCES AND FALLEN PALM TREES.
LAKE OKECHOBEE WEST END STATION.
BELLE GLADE FAWN STATION.
LAKE OKEECHOBEE CENTER STATION - SUSTAINED WINDS 42 MPH WITH GUST 49 MPH.

PICTURES AND VIDEO RECEIVED OF DAMAGE TO FENCES, SHEDS, AND TREES IN LOXAHATCHEE IN THE AREA OF ROYAL PALM BEACH BOULEVARD AND 42ND ROAD N. ANALYSIS OF EVIDENCE AND RADAR DATA SUGGEST THE LIKELIHOOD OF A LOCALIZED AREA OF MICROBURST-ORIGINATED WINDS OF 60-70 MPH.
REPORT OF LARGE TREES DOWN AROUND SE 27TH DR.
BUOY STATION VAKF1 VIRGINIA KEY, FL REPORTED A WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS (40 MPH) AT 3:36 AM AS A STRONG THUNDERSTORM MOVED OFFSHORE.
MESONET STATION XKBS BISCAYNE BAY LIGHT 20 REPORTED A WIND GUST OF 44 KNOTS (51 MPH) AT 3:37AM AS A STRONG THUNDERSTORM MOVED THROUGH THE AREA. SITE ELEVATION IS 20 FEET.
MESONET STATION XGVT GOVERNMENT CUT REPORTED A WIND GUST OF 38 KNOTS (44 MPH) AT 4:00 AM AS A STRONG THUNDERSTORM MOVED THROUGH THE AREA. SITE ELEVATION IS 75 FEET.
HAIL UP TO SIZE OF QUARTERS AT HOME OF NWS EMPLOYEE IN WEST MIRAMAR.
OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE MEASURES HAIL UP TO 1.25 INCHES IN DIAMETER.
HAIL UP TO 2 INCHES DIAMETER.
PALM BEACH COUNTY SKYWARN REPORTS UP TO QUARTER SIZE HAIL AT PROSPERTY FARMS AT NORTHLAKE BLVD.

EOC REPORTS WIND DAMAGE IN PALM BEACH GARDENS, WITH REPORTS OF TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN BLOCKING WESTBOUND LANES OF ALTERNATE A1A. CURRENTLY UNKNOWN IF DAMAGE WAS FROM STRAIGHT LINE WINDS, OR A TORNADO.

VARIOUS MEMBERS OF THE BROADCAST MEDIA SENT IN VIDEO FOOTAGE OF A WATERSPOUT BRIEFLY MOVING ONSHORE JUST SOUTH OF THE BOCA INLET (OCEAN TOWERS). SEVERAL UMBRELLAS AND CHAIRS WERE VISIBLY PICKED UP IN THE AIR BY THE CIRCULATION BEFORE THE CIRCULATION DISSIPATED SHORTLY AFTERWARDS.

OFF DUTY NWS METEOROLOGIST ON HOLLYWOOD BEACH REPORTED A WATER SPOUT A FEW MILES OFFSHORE WITH A SHOWER THAT WAS JUST OFFSHORE. BROADCAST MEDIA ALSO SHOWED A SPRAY RING VISIBLE WITH THE WATERSPOUT WITH A DURATION OF SEVERAL MINUTES.

BROADCAST MEDIA AERIAL COVERAGE OF LANDFALLING WATERSPOUT ON HOLLYWOOD BEACH, JUST EAST OF THE INTERSECTION OF HAYES STREET AND N SURF ROAD. VIDEO FOOTAGE SHOWED A CIRCULATION BRIEFLY MOVING ONSHORE WITH CHAIRS AND AN UMBRELLA BEING PICKED UP FROM THE BEACH. TORNADO WARNING WAS ISSUED AT 12:44PM.
MESONET STATION 1918W MEASURED A GUST TO 43 MPH.
MEMBER OF THE MEDIA SHARED AN IMAGE OF SEVERAL LARGE TREE BRANCHES DOWNED BY WINDS IN EXCESS OF 50 MPH WITH A STRONG THUNDERSTORM MONDAY NIGHT.
SOCIAL MEDIA POST WITH HAIL COVERING ENTIRE YARD. VARYING HAIL SIZE 0.5-1.5.
REPORT FROM MPING: HALF DOLLAR (1.25 IN.).
PHOTO REPORT TAKEN OF GOLF BALL SIZED HAIL IN PALM BEACH GARDENS.

CALLER REPORTED A FEW SHINGLES BLOWN OFF ROOFS, SCREEN PATIO BLOWN DOWN, TREE LIMBS OF SMALL TO MEDIUM SIZE DOWN, AND POWER OUTAGES IN THE VICINITY OF THE GOLFVIEW HARBOR APARTMENTS NEAR SOUTHWEST 23RD AVENUE IN BOYNTON BEACH. FOOTAGE PROVIDED BY A LOCAL BROADCAST METEOROLOGIST ALSO SHOW A CIRCULATION EVIDENT AT THE SURFACE WITH SMALL DEBRIS (LEAFS AND BRANCHES) ROTATING AROUND IN A TORNADIC MOTION. ADDITIONAL FOOTAGE ON SOCIAL MEDIA SHOW A DEFINED ROTATING FUNNEL DESCENDING FROM THE BASE OF THE ACCOMPANYING THUNDERSTORM. A COUPLET IS ALSO IDENTIFIABLE ON TPBI VELOCITY SCANS DURING THE SAME TIME FRAME. BASED ON RADAR, THE MOTION OF THE TORNADO WAS FROM WEST TO EAST.
LAKE OKEECHOBEE NORTH END SFWMD REPORTED A WIND GUST OF 44 MPH AT 3:45PM AS SEVERE THUNDERSTORM MOVED ACROSS THE LAKE.
TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED QUARTER SIZE HAIL AND HEAVY RAIN.
NUMEROUS PUBLIC SPOTTER REPORTS OF GOLF BALL SIZED HAIL IN JUPITER FARMS.
HAM OPERATOR REPORTED PALM TREES DOWN NEAR PALM BEACH GARDENS HOSPITAL NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF BURNS ROAD AND ALTERNATIVE A1A. REPORT WAS SENT IN AT 5:17 PM VIA NWSCHAT.

NWS STORM SURVEY: TORNADO WAS AT LEAST AN EF-1 OF 100 MPH, AND A FINAL DETERMINATION WILL LIKELY BE MADE TOMORROW. THE STARTING POINT WAS EAST OF I-95 JUST SOUTH OF PALM BEACH GARDENS MEDICAL CENTER, WITH A NE TRACK ALL THE WAY TO NEAR THE PGA BOULEVARD/US 1 INTERSECTION, THEN FINISHING UP ON US 1 ABOUT 1/3 MILE NORTH OF PGA BOULEVARD.
HAM OPERATOR REPORTS TREES DOWN AND POWER OUTAGES NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF PGA BLVD AND ELLISON WILSON ROAD. REPORT WAS SENT IN AT 5:21PM VIA NWS CHAT.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TSTM WND DMG REPORT FROM 1 E WELLINGTON. PBSCO RECEIVED TWO CALLS AT 3:36PM OF A POWER LINE DOWN AT THE INTERSECTION OF SOUTH RAMBLING DRIVE AND SOUTH RAMBLING DRIVE CIRCLE. THIS OCCURRED AS A SEVERE THUNDERSTORM WAS MOVING THROUGH THE AREA. PBSCO REPORTS THAT THE DOWNED LINE HAS BEEN FIXED AT THIS TIME.

PBSCO RECEIVED TWO CALLS AT 4:36PM OF A POWER LINE DOWN AT THE INTERSECTION OF SOUTH RAMBLING DRIVE AND SOUTH RAMBLING DRIVE CIRCLE. THIS OCCURRED AS A SEVERE THUNDERSTORM WAS MOVING THROUGH THE AREA. PBSCO REPORTS THAT THE DOWNED LINE HAS BEEN FIXED AT THIS TIME.

HAM RADIO OPERATOR PASSED ALONG A REPORT FROM A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER OF A LARGE TREE AND POWERLINES DOWN ON LANTANA ROAD JUST EAST OF THE INTERSECTION WITH JOG ROAD. TIME ESTIMATION IS 3:59 PM AS THE SEVERE THUNDERSTORM MOVED THROUGH THE AREA.
BROADCAST METEOROLOGIST SHARED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA, SEVERAL TREES DOWN ON THE STREET AS WELL AS FENCE DAMAGE IN NORTHERN CLEWISTON NEAR PONCE DE LEON AVENUE. RADAR ESTIMATED TO HAVE TAKEN PLACE AT 4PM.

HENDRY COUNTY EM ADVISES OF A LOCAL STORM REPORT IN CLEWISTON. CALLER STATES THAT ROOF DAMAGE OCCURRED AT CLEWISTON NURSING AND REHABILITATION CENTER DUE TO THUNDERSTORM WINDS. NO INJURIES OR FATALITIES WERE REPORTED. CLEWISTON FD, PD, AND EMS ARE ON-SCENE AND CURRENTLY ASSISTING WITH AN EVACUATION OF THE BUILDING. NO STATE ASSISTANCE IS REQUESTED AT THIS TIME.
EARTH NETWORKS MESONET BLLGL GLADES CENTRAL COMMUNITY HIGH SCHOOL REPORTED A WIND GUST OF 62 MPH AT 4:54 PM AS A SEVERE THUNDERSTORM MOVED THROUGH THE AREA.

*** 1 INJ *** CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 2 NW GOULDS. PRELIMINARY NWS STORM SURVEY INFORMATION: A THUNDERSTORM PRODUCED A SHORT-LIVED EF-0 TORNADO STARTING AROUND SW 192ND ST THAT TRAVELED SOUTH ALONG SW 147TH AVE UNTIL ALMOST SW 200TH ST. SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED AND/OR UPROOTED. A SEMI TRACTOR TRAILER WAS KNOCKED ON ITS SIDE BY THE TORNADO NEAR SW 196TH ST AND THE DRIVER WAS INJURED. TIMES ESTIMATED OFF RADAR FROM 1:20 PM TO 1:22 PM EDT.

*** 1 INJ *** PRELIMINARY NWS STORM SURVEY INFORMATION: A THUNDERSTORM PRODUCED A SHORT-LIVED EF-0 TORNADO STARTING AROUND SW 196TH ST THAT TRAVELED SOUTH ALONG SW 147TH AVE UNTIL ALMOST SW 200TH ST.
